{{Infobox character
| name = The Wanderer
| aliases = Wanderer
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = room guardian
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[The Wanderer Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Wanderer, also known as [[Valin]], is a legendary figure who was once human but evolved into something more. He arrived in [[the House]] through a sudden, violent flash of light and sound, appearing as if he had been cast from another location. Upon his arrival, he claimed [[the House]] and named it [[Valinhall]]. Through his own expertise and the study of collected texts, he created a key that allows for travel between his original world and this one. He also forged keys for the twelve founding members of the [[Dragon Army]] and established a [[workshop]] where he developed various inventions, such as the Dragon's Fangs. While he was once considered the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]], he has noted that this status fades over time, leaving him feeling more like a guardian of the rooms. Physically, the Wanderer is described with striking and unsettling features. His eyes lack any white, consisting instead of solid blackness with irises that gleam like metallic silver [[steel]]. His appearance can vary from a hideous figure in stained, dirty robes with a long beard filled with insects to a tall man in travel-ready clothing carrying a large book. His presence has the power to physically alter his surroundings, causing the environment to reshape itself into structures like polished wood and carved beams. He is a master of his domain, having explored the deepest reaches of [[Valinhall]] that others fear to enter, and he is known to wield a significant [[sword]] that commands great respect.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Wanderer]] arrived in [[Valinhall]] by falling into the entry hall with a flash of light and a great roar.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Wanderer]] was known as [[Valin]] among men.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Wanderer]] forged the [[Demon Fang]] [[Azura]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After crafting the Dragon’s Fangs within the forge, the Wanderer vanished from sight. Although a grave exists for this figure in [[the House]], it is known that they were the original owner of [[Mithra]]. The Wanderer once provided insight regarding metaphysical distance and the connection between a [[Territory]] and its inherent powers. While the [[Valinhall Travelers]] are destined to encounter them again, the term Wanderer is also used to refer to [[Kai]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]] made the Dragon’s Fangs in the forge.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]] disappeared.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]] has a grave in [[the House]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]] is the original owner of [[Mithra]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]] spoke of metaphysical distance and the link between a [[Territory]] and its powers.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Travelers]] are set to face the Wanderer once again.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]] is referred to as a Wanderer.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Wanderer]] was once human but became something more.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Wanderer]] is no longer an [[Incarnation]] and serves as a room guardian.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Wanderer]] once asked [[Simon]] what he wants.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"He fell in the entry hall with a flash of light and a great roar; not as if he came willingly, but as if he was cast here from another place."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Among men, the Wanderer was known as Valin."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“I'm nothing more than a room guardian, like Makko or Kortali.”<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
